message:
fix(stream): fail-closed repoll guards and TryStreamError type for stream combinators

Add terminal-state tracking to Chain, Enumerate, and Skip so that
polling after Ready(None) returns a cached None rather than re-driving
the inner stream. This prevents subtle bugs where a completed stream
combinator is accidentally repolled and either panics in the inner
stream or silently produces unexpected items.

Introduce TryStreamError<E> enum to wrap errors from try-stream
combinators (TryCollect, TryFold, TryForEach), distinguishing between
inner errors (TryStreamError::Inner) and repoll-after-completion
violations (TryStreamError::PolledAfterCompletion). This replaces the
previous assert-based guards with error-returning fail-closed behavior
that is testable and does not crash the runtime.

Changes by file:
- chain.rs: add `done` field, guard poll_next and size_hint, add
  EmptyThenPanics test helper and repoll regression tests
- enumerate.rs: add `done` field, guard poll_next and size_hint,
  add repoll regression tests for both empty and non-empty streams
- skip.rs: add `done` field, guard poll_next and size_hint, add
  repoll and size_hint-after-completion regression tests
- try_stream.rs: introduce TryStreamError<E> with Display/Error/From
  impls, update TryCollect/TryFold/TryForEach to return
  TryStreamError-wrapped errors, convert assert! guards to
  error-returning fail-closed pattern, add repoll regression tests
- mod.rs: re-export TryStreamError from stream module
- zip.rs: remove trailing blank line

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
